citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
The facility has not adequately communicated with the attending veterinarian to provide information regarding the
progressing locomotion difficulties of two, approximately seven-year-old, white lions. At the time of inspection, the lions
were observed having trouble standing and walking. The lions were observed to be extremely weak, trembling in the legs,
staggering when walking, and are occasionally collapsing to the ground. The facility makes daily observations but had not
contacted the attending veterinarian regarding the progression of the symptoms. The attending veterinarian confirmed
that he had last been at the facility in the fall of 2025. The attending veterinarian stated that at the time of his last visit the
lions did not exhibit the symptoms observed at the time of inspection. The attending veterinarian has not been contacted
since his last visit to evaluate the health of the two lions.
Each dealer or exhibitor shall establish and maintain programs of adequate veterinary care that include: Daily observation
of all animals to assess their health and well-being; Provided, however, That daily observation of animals may be
accomplished by someone other than the attending veterinarian; and Provided, further, That a mechanism of direct and
frequent communication is required so that timely and accurate information on problems of animal health, behavior, and
well-being is conveyed to the attending veterinarian
To be corrected by noon on 4-10-2026, local time.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(3)  Direct
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
The facility has not adequately communicated with the attending veterinarian to provide information regarding the 
progressing locomotion difficulties of two, approximately seven-year-old, white lions. At the time of inspection, the lions 
were observed having trouble standing and walking. The lions were observed to be extremely weak, trembling in the legs, 
staggering when walking, and are occasionally collapsing to the ground. The facility makes daily observations but had not 
contacted the attending veterinarian regarding the progression of the symptoms. The attending veterinarian confirmed 
that he had last been at the facility in the fall of 2025. The attending veterinarian stated that at the time of his last visit the 
lions did not exhibit the symptoms observed at the time of inspection. The attending veterinarian has not been contacted 
since his last visit to evaluate the health of the two lions. 
 
Each dealer or exhibitor shall establish and maintain programs of adequate veterinary care that include: Daily observation 
of all animals to assess their health and well-being; Provided, however, That daily observation of animals may be 
accomplished by someone other than the attending veterinarian; and Provided, further, That a mechanism of direct and 
frequent communication is required so that timely and accurate information on problems of animal health, behavior, and 
well-being is conveyed to the attending veterinarian  
 
To be corrected by noon on 4-10-2026, local time. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
